scope_boundary: Circle contract-pack archive verification only. The ASI Stack repository now carries a pinned public-safe Circle contract-pack artifact from commit 63b0f511 with 9 archived contracts, 4 acceptance-policy receipts, public_safe_fixture status, pack fingerprint df673f8a661fc89a26372685986c92f2221aaa617d6738fce5c2a76bd5d0eeae, raw and stable JSON digests, public-safety hygiene checks, five expected-invalid mutation controls, and transition record circle_contract_pack_archive_no_change.json. This does not rerun Circle Lean, does not prove deployed proof-contract transport, does not prove model quality, context length, runtime speed, memory scaling, deployment safety, transfer, safety, or ASI, and does not promote the Circle chapter core claim.
transition_reason: The archive materially improves reproducibility because reviewers can inspect and validate the Circle contract-pack artifact inside this public repository instead of relying only on a local summary or a single receipt fixture. The evidence still supports only archive integrity and boundary discipline: it is not a fresh Circle replay, not a deployed proof-contract transport trace, and not a workload or model-quality result, so the proper support-state effect is blocks_promotion.
required_artifacts: fresh public Circle replay or stable external release replay
Circle Lean proof-target rerun or independently archived proof bundle
deployed proof-contract transport implementation or consumer integration trace
workload, baseline, metric, and report for any downstream model-quality, context-length, transfer, speed, or memory-scaling claim
accepted evidence-transition record for any narrowed upward support movement

contract fingerprint mismatch mutation is rejected
rejected acceptance-policy report mutation is rejected
acceptance-report pack mismatch mutation is rejected
fresh Circle replay in this repository remains unclaimed
no chapter core claim is promoted

support-state promotion without a separate accepted upward transition
promotion_burden: Move above argument only for a narrowed Circle archive, replay, or proof-contract transport claim after a fresh public Circle replay or independently archived proof bundle, repository-owned replay or verification command, downstream consumer integration evidence, workload/baseline metrics for any model-facing claim, and independent review are recorded with a separate accepted upward transition.
